## Web context (cite URLs when used)
- National Sporting Club - Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Sporting_Club): # National Sporting Club. The **National Sporting Club** (**NSC**) was a private members' sporting club founded in London on 5 March 1891 by John Fleming and Arthur Frederick Bettinson. Operating from premises at 43 King Street, Covent Garden, it became the principal centre for elite professional boxing in Britain during the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries. During the early twentieth century, eligibility for British championship contests was effectively limited to white boxers through administrative practice rather than formal statute, a restriction that remained in place for the

- The National Sporting Club Past and Present | Oxfam Shop (https://onlineshop.oxfam.org.uk/the-national-sporting-club-past-and-present/product/HD_303323292): A history of the famous, then nearly 300 years old, National Sporting Club, renowned for its promotion of the art and science of boxing, and based in Covent
- The National Sporting Club - Books (https://www.amazon.com/National-Sporting-Club-Present-Classic/dp/1334227365): This book chronicles the history of the National Sporting Club in London, from 1664 to 1856, and then from its rebirth in 1891 to 1901.
- National Sporting Club (https://www.londonremembers.com/subjects/national-sporting-club): Founded by John Fleming and AF "Peggy" Bettinson. First president Hugh Cecil Lowther, Fifth Earl of Lonsdale. It became the home of modern glove boxing.

rounds.story: Walk just 12 meters straight ahead from the old singing rooms — and you’ll stand before **43 King Street**, a building that hosted not just plays but prizefights 130 years before Fight Club was a glint in Chuck Palahniuk’s eye. This is the footprint of the **National Sporting Club**, London’s 19th-century nexus for elite boxing, where white-only bouts were held after dinner, before brandy, in the same room where patrons now sip espresso (if there’s a café here — data’s vague). The alleyway vibe persists: just across the street, the **Middle Earth** club once pulsed with 1960s counterculture, [📍map](https://www.google.com/maps/search/National+Sporting+Club/@51.5121,-0.1239,17z/) [📖wiki](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Sporting_Club). Skip the tour — unless you’re a pugilist with a taste for history.
